_meta.name: OpenClaw Gateway Adapter Template
_meta.status: Sprint 2+ — not available in current Paperclip version
_meta.description: OpenClaw gateway adapter configuration template for controlled external API access. Paperclip's openclaw-gateway adapter is listed in the Docker image but not yet documented as production-ready. This template is prepared for activation when gateway support is confirmed stable.

agentTemplate.name: gateway-tender-intel
agentTemplate.title: Gateway-Enabled Tender Intelligence
agentTemplate.capabilities: Controlled external API access for tender portal monitoring, government data retrieval, and market intelligence gathering via OpenClaw gateway

dataClassification.description: Controls what data can be sent through the gateway to external APIs
rules: No client-confidential data may be sent to external APIs
No financial data from Xero may be forwarded externally
Tender search queries may include sector keywords only, not internal capability details
Graph API calls are limited to SharePoint file operations and Teams notifications
All gateway requests must be logged with timestamp, destination, and response code
Request and response bodies are logged for audit (exclude auth headers)
Logs are retained for 90 days minimum
Weekly gateway usage summary is generated automatically

activationChecklist: Verify Paperclip openclaw-gateway adapter is production-ready (check release notes)
Set up OpenClaw gateway instance with the River routing policy
Configure allowed origins to match River's integration endpoints only
Obtain OpenClaw API key and configure as a Paperclip secret
Create the agent using this template via POST /api/companies/{companyId}/agents
Write instruction files to the agent's instructionsRootPath
Test gateway connectivity to each allowed origin
Trigger a test heartbeat and verify gateway routing works correctly
Monitor gateway logs for the first week to validate policy enforcement
Set a low initial budget (2000 cents = $20/month) until usage patterns are established
